codeReview.summary: The commit adds substantial M4 cross-flow integration coverage, but it does not fully verify VAL-CROSS-035 as defined in the contract (multi-relationship entities requiring foreign-key/join-table and nested-route behavior), so the feature cannot be accepted as complete.

issues.description: VAL-CROSS-035 is under-verified: the multi-relationship test only checks *_id columns and index names, but never asserts migration-level foreign key/join-table behavior or nested route generation. The contract explicitly requires REFERENCES and/or join table evidence plus nested routes for multiple relationships, so this test can pass while required behavior is missing.
VAL-CROSS-033 column checks are global over the full SQL blob instead of scoped per table. Reused column names (for example id) can satisfy assertions even if a specific table definition is wrong.

The "generated TypeScript files compile" section uses brace/semicolon heuristics rather than invoking tsc in-test. This is weaker than the contract expectation of compiler-backed syntax validation and may miss real TypeScript errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Contract-level expectations for browser-based concurrent-page validation are not enforced by shared guidance, which allowed VAL-CROSS-085 to be treated as API concurrency only.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:1252-1255 requires page navigation during active pipeline execution (agent-browser evidence), while test/m4_cross_flow_test.exs:712-757 validates only API endpoint responses.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 93c44f0. The added tests are broad, but a blocking contract gap remains: VAL-CROSS-035 requirements for multi-relationship foreign-key/join-table and nested-route verification are not actually asserted, so this feature review fails.
